I'll own core.py exclusively: add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts via _make_sub_context, validate in close()/__exit__, raise RuntimeError if children still open. If you don't end up needing changes in core.py's __exit__/close beyond what's already there, this keeps us fully disjoint. Let me know if that works or if you specifically need to change __exit__/close() logic in core.py.

note: Heads up: I just checked and your shell_completion.py edits are NOT appearing in my working copy (git diff shows zero changes there) even after your DONE message - looks like our sandboxes/filesystems are actually isolated per-agent, not shared, despite the 'same codebase' framing. my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished my feature (nested context validation). core.py: added validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (default False), tracks open child contexts created via _make_sub_context, raises RuntimeError in close() if any are still open, removes closed children from parent tracking, and _make_sub_context now propagates validate_nesting to children. shell_completion.py: added validate_nesting=False explicitly to all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context (minimal addition only - I did not touch your 'with' wrapping resource-leak logic since our filesystems are apparently isolated per-agent and this was purely to make my own patch.txt self-contained/testable). Full test suite (558 tests) passes. All done on my end.
